# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.91.0/http/server.ts";
interface RequestObject extends Deno.RequestEvent {}  // extend the interface if more fields are added in future to include them when requesting data, e.g., time: number; userAgent?: string etc... depending on your use case    -- not sure about this part yet from my current knowledge and understanding of typescript
import { Permissions }from 'https://deno.land/x/permissions@v0.9.2/mod.ts';  // permission check toolkit, denops permissions or similar for our project would be better option in context with other tools used here (like user request)    -- not sure about this part yet from my current knowledge and understanding of typescript
const urlList: Array<string> = Deno.args;   /* we need to extract these urls dynamically depending on the number/type os command line arguments */  // This is a list or array containing URLs provided as parameters during execution, e.g., ["/url1", "/url2"]
// Function definition with type annotations for better code readability and typing hints     -- not sure about this part yet from my current knowledge and understanding of typescript   / deno deploy functions should also be added in the future here to make use case specific scenarios more efficient    // possibly related function definitions would go into a module or separate service
function getData(request: RequestObject): Response {  /* we need type definition for this as well (possibly include headers, status and content types if any) */   }     -- not sure about the exact requirements here from my current understanding of typescript    // possibly related error handling logic can be incorporated to make code more robust
export async function denoDeployFetcher(request: RequestObject): Promise<Response> {  /* we need type definition for this as well */   }     -- not sure about the exact requirements here from my current understanding of typescript    // possibly related event-driven architecture can be incorporated to handle multiple request in a non blocking way
// Server setup with routing and middleware like deno deploy functions would go into another file or module, but for now let's just set up our server (listeners)  -- not sure about this part yet from my current knowledge and understanding of typescript    // possibly related listeners to these async function requests could be added in the future here as per use case specific scenarios
serve(denoDeployFetcher), () => console.log("Server running on port ", Deno.addr().port);   -- not sure about this part yet from my current knowledge and understanding of typescript    // possibly related logging can also be incorporated to debug or log the requests/responses for better insight, e.g., denops logs toolkit could come in handy
